Front: Why are there exactly 6 RAG evals, not 3 and not 9?
Back: 3 components (Q, C, A). Form ordered pairs of two DISTINCT components: 3 unordered pairs x 2 directions = 6. Not 3 because direction matters (A|C differs from C|A). Not 9 because you exclude self-conditioning (Q|Q, C|C, A|A are meaningless), i.e. drop the diagonal of the 3x3 grid.

Front: Name the 6 RAG evals by tier.
Back: Tier 1 foundation: Retrieval Precision & Recall. Tier 2 Primary: Context Relevance (C|Q), Faithfulness/Groundedness (A|C), Answer Relevance (A|Q). Tier 3 Advanced: Context Support Coverage (C|A), Question Answerability (Q|C), Self-Containment (Q|A).

Front: Distinguish A|C from C|A, and what each implicates.
Back: A|C (faithfulness): is the answer fully grounded in the context (no claims beyond it)? Low A|C = generator hallucinating. C|A (context support coverage): does the context contain enough, focused info to back every claim? High A|C but low C|A = retriever under-fetched. A|C points at the generator; C|A points at the retriever.

Front: Why does medical RAG demand high faithfulness (A|C) while customer service weights answer relevance (A|Q)?
Back: It is about which failure mode costs most. Medicine: a fluent-but-ungrounded claim causes patient harm and liability, so guard grounding (A|C). Customer service: an unhelpful or off-target answer frustrates the user, so guard whether the answer addresses the question (A|Q).

Front: RAG debugging: user says 'the answer is wrong/made up.' What do you check and in what order?
Back: Check faithfulness (A|C) first. If the answer contradicts or exceeds the context, it is a generator/hallucination problem. If the answer is faithful but still bad, move upstream to Context Relevance (C|Q) to see if the retriever pulled irrelevant context. One complaint, two checks, failure localized to a component.

Front: Longest Consecutive Sequence: how do you get O(n), and why is the predecessor check essential?
Back: Put nums in a set. Only start counting a run from a sequence START (num-1 not in set), then walk num+1 upward. Without the guard you would re-walk a run from every member -> O(n^2) (worst case [1..n]). With it, each element is consumed by exactly one walk, so total inner-loop work is O(n) by amortized counting.
